title: Ukraine: Over 3,000 attacks on healthcare since full-scale Russian invasion
content: The World Health Organization (WHO) has verified more than 3,000 attacks on healthcare in Ukraine since Russia launched its full-scale invasion in February 2022, the UN agency reported on Friday.
snippet: The World Health Organization (WHO) has verified more than 3,000 attacks on healthcare in Ukraine since Russia launched its full-scale invasion in February 2022, the UN agency reported on Friday.
sourceTitle: UN News - Global perspective Human stories | Europe
sourceDescription: UN News - Global perspective Human stories | Europe

title: More than 70 civilians killed in Ukraine in less than a week
content: At least 70 civilians have been killed and more than 500 injured across Ukraine since the start of May, UN human rights monitors said on Wednesday, as waves of attacks hit cities across the country and humanitarian workers struggled to reach communities near the frontline.

title: World News in Brief: AI diagnostics, humanitarian deal for DR Congo, rights abuse allegations in Belarus, Ukraine children bear heaviest burden
content: New data shows that nearly three in four countries in Europe now use Artificial Intelligence in their health services to make a diagnosis.
snippet: New data shows that nearly three in four countries in Europe now use Artificial Intelligence in their health services to make a diagnosis.

title: SECURITY COUNCIL LIVE: Emergency briefing on Ukraine amid escalating Russian attacks
content: The Security Council meets in emergency session at 3pm to address the escalating conflict in Ukraine. The open briefing follows a formal request from Kyiv citing a surge in Russian aerial bombardments, including devastating strikes on the city of Dnipro. Ukraine has reported that between late March and mid-April, more than 5,000 drones and missiles were launched, killing dozens of civilians and injuring hundreds more. Follow full meetings coverage below and UN News app users can follow here.
snippet: The Security Council meets in emergency session at 3pm to address the escalating conflict in Ukraine. The open briefing follows a formal request from Kyiv citing a surge in Russian aerial bombardments
sourceTitle: UN News - Global perspective Human stories | Europe
sourceDescription: UN News - Global perspective Human stories | Europe

title: Deadly Russian strikes on Ukrainians ‘simply trying to live their lives’ must stop, says senior UN official
content: The latest wave of Russian attacks on Ukrainian civilians in Dnipro, Kyiv and Odesa, which killed and injured scores of civilians, was roundly condemned by Matthias Schmale, the UN Humanitarian Coordinator in the country.

title: European Union support for the UN ‘more vital than ever’, Security Council hears
content: A senior United Nations official on Monday hailed the European Union (EU) as “a major economic and diplomatic actor and a strong advocate of multilateralism.”

title: Kosovo mission head warns ‘mistrust’ threatens post-election stability
content: Despite successful legislative elections in Kosovo late last year, a “delicate equilibrium” persists as deep divisions remain over the future of the United Nations presence in the region.
snippet: Despite successful legislative elections in Kosovo late last year, a “delicate equilibrium” persists as deep divisions remain over the future of the United Nations presence in the region.

title: Deminers race to keep up with military technology
content: In conflict zones where new technologies are making landmines more dangerous, deminers must innovate at the same pace to avoid being left behind, a leading UN mines expert has told UN News.

title: Ukraine: danger is only increasing, warns UN human rights office
content: More than four years since Russia’s full-scale invasion of Ukraine, the “danger is only increasing”, particularly from attack drones, a top UN human rights official warned on Thursday.
snippet: More than four years since Russia’s full-scale invasion of Ukraine, the “danger is only increasing”, particularly from attack drones, a top UN human rights official warned on Thursday.

title: Ukraine violence ‘worse than ever’, Security Council hears
content: More than four years after Russia launched its full-scale invasion of Ukraine, “the violence is worse than ever” and far from abating, the head of UN political and peacebuilding affairs said on Monday in a briefing to the Security Council.
snippet: More than four years after Russia launched its full-scale invasion of Ukraine, “the violence is worse than ever” and far from abating, the head of UN political and peacebuilding affairs said on Monday

title: SECURITY COUNCIL LIVE: Ukraine in spotlight as Russian strikes surge
content: The Security Council is met for an open briefing on Ukraine, requested by European members following Kyiv’s 18 March letter citing a surge in Russian strikes. Inside the country, attacks continue to take a heavy toll: since 19 March, at least 25 civilians have reportedly been killed and more than 130 injured, including children, particularly in Donetsk, Sumy and Zaporizhzhia. We’ll have live coverage below, app users can follow here – and for all our key meetings coverage each day, go here.

sourceTitle: UN News - Global perspective Human stories | Europe

title: Forests are more than a resource: How Belarus protects its vital ecosystem
content: Forests cover more than 40 per cent of Belarus. At the same time, the country recorded twice as many forest fires last year compared to the year before.

title: Russia deportation of Ukraine children is crime against humanity: independent probe
content: Scores of Ukrainian children are still missing after being deported far and wide across Russia and occupied territories while their families continue to search for them, human rights investigators said on Thursday.

content: Four years after Russia’s full-scale invasion of Ukraine, many people are trying to overcome deep-rooted trauma and rebuild what has been lost.

title: ‘The trauma will stay with me for life,’ says survivor of sexual violence in war-torn Ukraine
content: One evening in war-ravaged southern Ukraine, a group of armed men entered Maryna’s home. She had nowhere to hide.

title: Ukraine: ‘Use every diplomatic tool to end this war’, top UN official tells Security Council
content: The international community must “use every diplomatic tool” to bring an end to the war in Ukraine, a senior UN official told the Security Council on Tuesday as Russia’s full-scale invasion entered a fifth year.

title: LIVE: UN marks fourth anniversary of Russia’s full-scale invasion of Ukraine
content: Four years after Russia launched its full-scale invasion of Ukraine, the UN is marking the day with high-level debate and renewed calls to end the war - including in the General Assembly which passed a resolution reaffirming its strong commitment to the sovereignty, independence and territorial integrity of Ukraine. The UN chief just told the Security Council diplomats must use every tool available to finally end the war. UN News App users can follow the coverage here. For full UN meetings coverage today and every day, go here.

sourceTitle: UN News - Global perspective Human stories | Europe

title: Ukraine wakes to more violence as Russia’s full-scale invasion enters fifth year
content: The full-scale invasion of Ukraine by Russian troops on 24 February 2022 shattered the peaceful aspirations of an entire continent, but war must never be the new normal, UN General Assembly President Annalena Baerbock said on Tuesday.

title: Myriad fragments, one tragedy: How four years of war changed Ukraine
content: Although fighting had been raging in the east of Ukraine since Russia's invasion of Crimea in 2014, most in the country did not believe all-out war would occur. With the full-scale invasion now reaching the four-year mark, many Ukrainians cannot believe it has gone on for so long, with no end in sight.

title: Ukraine: $588 billion recovery cost over the next 10 years
content: Reconstruction and recovery in Ukraine will cost almost $588 billion over the next decade, or nearly three times the estimated GDP in 2025, as housing, energy and other critical sectors continue to come under fire from Russian forces.

title: Ukraine’s women at breaking point after four years of war as attacks on energy, healthcare continue – UN humanitarians
content: Four years into Russia’s full-scale invasion, millions in Ukraine struggle to keep the lights on and heat their homes, with the crisis taking a particular toll on women, humanitarians warned on Friday.

title: Four years of war in Ukraine: Childhood has ‘moved underground’, displacement continues – UN humanitarians
content: Civilian suffering shows no sign of letting up in Ukraine as the four-year-mark of Russia’s full-scale invasion nears amid attacks on energy infrastructure, blackouts and freezing temperatures, UN humanitarians warned on Tuesday.

title: As conditions worsen in Ukraine, refugees struggle to return
content: As Ukraine prepares to enter the fifth year of the full-scale Russian invasion on 24 February, UN monitors say harm to civilians has “demonstrably worsened”, while energy attacks and freezing temperatures are making it harder for displaced families to return.

title: Ukraine: UN aid convoy reaches frontlines in Dnipro
content: A UN humanitarian convoy reached frontline communities in Ukraine’s Dnipro region on Wednesday, delivering critical medical and hygiene supplies as fighting continues to take a heavy toll on civilians and infrastructure across the country.

title: Measles cases drop in 2025 across Europe and Central Asia, but outbreak risks remain
content: Measles cases across Europe and Central Asia declined by 75 per cent in 2025 compared to 2024, according to preliminary data released on Wednesday by the World Health Organization (WHO), which warned of remaining outbreak risks.

content: The President of the United Nations General Assembly appealed on Tuesday for Europe to protect the international rules-based system, defend the truth in the face of fake news and other falsehoods, and support UN reform.

title: Ukraine war keeps nuclear safety on a knife-edge, UN watchdog warns
content: Attacks on Ukraine’s power system highlight how the ongoing war threatens the safety of the country’s nuclear facilities, the head of the International Atomic Energy Agency (IAEA) warned on Friday.

title: Ukraine: Civilians injured, miners killed, in separate Russian attacks
content: A fresh wave of Russian strikes overnight across Ukraine injured several people and left thousands “without heat in the heart of winter,” the UN Humanitarian Coordinator in the country said on Tuesday.

title: From Cape Town to London, Deo Kato runs to challenge racism and reclaim the migration narrative
content: Step by step, mile by mile, Deo Kato ran his way across a continent — and beyond. After a year and a half on the road, the Ugandan British runner and campaigner has become the first person to run from Cape Town to London.

title: UN watchdog warns Ukraine war remains world’s biggest threat to nuclear safety
content: The war in Ukraine remains the world’s biggest threat to nuclear safety as a fifth year of combat looms, the head of the UN nuclear watchdog warned on Friday, citing continued risks to power supplies at nuclear sites vulnerable to fighting nearby.
